# GreenBoost Windows KMDF Driver — CMake build config
#
# The KMDF driver is typically built via the WDK MSBuild integration,
# not vanilla CMake. This CMakeLists.txt generates a Visual Studio
# solution that can be used as a starting point, but the canonical
# build uses the WDK project template.
#
# For WDK builds, use the .vcxproj generated by the WDK "KMDF Driver"
# template and add these source files to it.

cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.20)

# Driver source files
set(DRIVER_SOURCES
    greenboost_win.c
)

set(DRIVER_HEADERS
    greenboost_win.h
    greenboost_ioctl_win.h
)

# Note: A real KMDF build requires the WDK toolchain which integrates
# via MSBuild, not CMake. This target is provided for IDE navigation
# and syntax checking. For actual compilation, use:
#   msbuild greenboost_win.vcxproj /p:Configuration=Release /p:Platform=x64
#
# Or import into Visual Studio with WDK installed.
if(WDK_ROOT)
    message(STATUS "WDK found at ${WDK_ROOT} — driver build enabled")
    # WDK integration would go here
else()
    message(STATUS "WDK not found — driver target is syntax-check only")
    # Create a static library target for IDE support / syntax checking
    add_library(greenboost_driver STATIC ${DRIVER_SOURCES} ${DRIVER_HEADERS})
    target_compile_definitions(greenboost_driver PRIVATE
        _KERNEL_MODE
        _AMD64_
        NTDDI_VERSION=0x0A000007  # Windows 10 20H1
        _WIN32_WINNT=0x0A00
    )
    # This won't actually link — it's for IDE navigation only
    set_target_properties(greenboost_driver PROPERTIES
        EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL TRUE
    )
endif()
